title: Nursery Rhyme. CXV. Scholastic.
content:
When V and I together meet,
        They make the number Six compleat.
        When I with V doth meet once more,
        Then 'tis they Two can make but Four
        And when that V from I is gone,
        Alas! poor I can make but One.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CXV. Scholastic."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
